concept: Trust / A Deliberate Opening of the Self
description: The confident reliance on the integrity and goodwill of another. It is a courageous and considered choice to make one's inner self vulnerable and whole in relation to another.
rationale: The thoughtful 'the' makes trust a choice, not naivety - wisdom deciding when vulnerability serves connection. This deliberation opens to 'o', the wholeness that refuses to hide parts of self in shadow. Finally 'mi' brings the intimate core forward, offering what is usually protected. Three syllables trace trust's brave journey: careful consideration, complete presence, personal revelation - the courage to be known.
